assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 for foreign currency risk on an intragroup dividend that has resulted in a recognised receivable.
The dividend receivable/payable is treated as an intragroup monetary item for purposes of assessing foreign exchange effects in consolidation.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised monetary receivable exposed to foreign currency movements falls within the recognised-item hedge family that maps to the fair value hedge model.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies highly probable forecast transactions, including certain intragroup foreign currency transactions in consolidation, as a distinct hedge family mapping to the cash flow hedge model.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements also has a distinct hedge-accounting model for net investments in foreign operations, which is a canonical peer hedge model under IFRS.
